name: Artefact Intelligence: AI-Personalized Museum Descriptions
shortDescription: Investigating how Large Language Models can adapt museum artefact descriptions to enhance visitor engagement using privacy-preserving personalization strategies.
fullDescription: This master's thesis investigates how LLMs can adapt museum artefact descriptions to enhance visitor engagement, and explores effective privacy-preserving personalization strategies for accommodating diverse visitor preferences and needs in AI adaptations. Using a Research through Design (RtD) approach and guided by The Museum Visitor Experience Model, a web-based prototype was designed and developed to explore the research questions.
outcomes: Findings showed that AI-adapted descriptions were rated as significantly more engaging than the originals.

name: LLMs for Conceptual Learning in Higher Education
shortDescription: Investigating the effectiveness of AI conversational tools like ChatGPT and custom GPTs in enhancing conceptual understanding in higher education.
fullDescription: This project investigates user experience and the effectiveness of AI-generated conversational tools, particularly Large Language Models (LLMs), in enhancing comprehension of conceptual subjects in educational contexts. The study utilized ChatGPT-4, GPTs, and a custom model trained on a specific subject. A total of 38 students across seven disciplines participated.
outcomes: ChatGPT and custom GPTs outperformed websites in enhancing comprehension and user experience.

name: GPTs for CBT-Based Public Speaking Anxiety
shortDescription: Exploring how ChatGPT can facilitate Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) to make treatment for public speaking anxiety more accessible.
fullDescription: Public speaking anxiety (PSA) is a recognized mental health challenge. CBT has proven effective in reducing this type of anxiety. The study explored people's acceptance of ChatGPT-assisted CBT, focusing on trust, subjective feelings, performance, and knowledge acquisition.
outcomes: The study provides insights into people's acceptance of AI-assisted CBT and its potential for mental health interventions.
